title: PCI-DSS 11.3 — Vulnerability programme: overdue scan cadence and unremediated high-severity
exclusions: Does NOT replace the scanner; measures cadence adherence (quarterly for external, internal as required) and remediation SLA for high/critical findings.
evidence: Saved search 'pci_11_3_scan_sla' (daily) emits per scope: scope, last_scan_at, sla_met, open_high_findings, mean_age_days, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI-DSS 11.3 External and internal vulnerabilities identified — directly evidenced.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI-DSS 11.3 (External and internal vulnerabilities identified) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.103: PCI-DSS 11.3 — Vulnerability programme: overdue scan cadence and unremediated high-severity.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.8 ICT risk identification — contributing.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.8 (Identification) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.103: PCI-DSS 11.3 — Vulnerability programme: overdue scan cadence and unremediated high-severity.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.103' running on index=vm sourcetype=tenable:sc:vuln, qualys:host; scan-scope lookup pci_scan_scope.csv., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A scope whose last_scan_at is 100 days ago with 3 open high findings aged 45 days returns sla_met=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A scope scanned within cadence with all findings <30 days old is absent.

spl:
index=vm sourcetype IN (tenable:sc:vuln,qualys:host) earliest=-120d
| stats latest(_time) AS last_scan_at count(eval(severity IN ("high","critical") AND state="open")) AS open_high_findings avg(eval(if(state="open" AND severity IN ("high","critical"),now()-first_seen,null))) AS mean_age_s BY scan_scope
| eval mean_age_days=round(mean_age_s/86400,0)
| lookup pci_scan_scope.csv scan_scope OUTPUT cadence_days sla_days
| eval sla_met=if(last_scan_at>=now()-cadence_days*86400 AND mean_age_days<=sla_days,1,0)
| where sla_met=0
| table scan_scope last_scan_at sla_met open_high_findings mean_age_days
description: Produces, per scan scope, the evidence required by PCI-DSS 11.3 — scans are on cadence and high/critical findings are remediated within SLA.
value: Closes the #1 open-finding reason at QSAs — 'the scan history was incomplete'. Continuous measurement is the intended replacement.
implementation: (1) Normalise scanner feeds; (2) maintain pci_scan_scope.csv (quarterly external, per-change internal); (3) schedule UC daily; (4) sla_met=0 opens a P2; (5) quarterly QSA report. Indexes required: `index=vm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=qualys:host`, `sourcetype=tenable:sc:vuln`.
visualization: Bullet chart of SLA adherence by scope, table of overdue scopes, single value 'scopes overdue'. Key fields: `last_scan_at`, `mean_age_days`, `mean_age_s`, `open_high_findings`, `scan_scope`, `sla_met`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t dc(Vulnerabilities.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - agg_value
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Vulnerabilities (CIM data model).
knownFalsePositives:
1. **Authorised compliance operations** — The compliance and governance team performs routine control testing, evidence gathering, and policy reviews that generate legitimate activity matching this use case's detection patterns. These operations are documented in the compliance programme calendar and should be excluded by the compliance-ops service account tag.

2. **Scheduled system maintenance windows** — Approved maintenance activities including patching, upgrades, and configuration changes produce expected control deviations during documented change windows that are pre-approved through the change advisory board (CAB).

3. **External audit and assessment activities** — During periodic audits (internal or external), assessors perform sampling, testing, and evidence collection activities that generate unusual access patterns covered by the engagement letter and audit notification.

4. **Third-party service provider operations** — Contracted service providers performing legitimate operations under signed agreements generate expected activity that may trigger monitoring rules during normal service delivery windows.

**Suppression mechanism:** Maintain a `compliance_exception_register` KV Store lookup containing approved maintenance windows, audit engagement periods, service provider identifiers, and compliance team service accounts. The correlation search joins this lookup and suppresses matches where `exception_status=approved` and the exception remains within its validity period.
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Indexes**: `vm` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Lookups**: `pci_scan_scope.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('pci_scan_scope.csv')`.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `11.3`. The control objective is: "Evidence that PCI-DSS 11.3 (External and internal vulnerabilities identified) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.103: PCI-DSS 11.3 — Vulnerability programme: overdue scan cadence and unremediated high-severity." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is bespoke for this UC — no single canonical TA parses every source the detection relies on. Route data into `vm` under the relevant sourcetypes, and document the ingestion path in the UC runbook so future engineers know where each field originates.


5 operational considerations apply to the rollout of this detection:


• **Hint 1:** Normalise scanner feeds. Field normalisation mismatches are one of the top silent-failure modes in enrichment-driven detections — invest in automated tests that assert key-match rates after every TA upgrade.

• **Hint 2:** maintain pci_scan_scope.csv (quarterly external, per-change internal). The lookup table identified here is a long-lived compliance artifact; version it in source control and track every change with a pull-request review.

• **Hint 3:** schedule UC daily. Schedule cadence is a tradeoff between regulatory latency and search-head load; document the chosen cadence and the reasoning in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 4:** sla_met=0 opens a P2. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 5:** quarterly QSA report. Report-generation cadence must match the regulatory reporting cycle; a monthly digest satisfies an annual review requirement but not a quarterly one.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "PCI-DSS 11.3 — Vulnerability programme: overdue scan cadence and unremediated high-severity," the aggregation exposes temporal structure that a single point-in-time reading cannot — the compliance question is about behaviour over a period, not at an instant.

This search enriches raw events with context from the lookup `pci_scan_scope.csv` — the authoritative Cardholder Data Environment (CDE) scope register. The raw event stream alone cannot answer the compliance question because it does not carry the authoritative business attributes (`cadence_days sla_days`) — these live in a register maintained outside the monitored system. The `| lookup` joins at search time on `scan_scope`, making the search results only as accurate as the lookup's freshness.

Lookup ownership and freshness: This is the single most common failure mode for lookup-based compliance searches. The `pci_scan_scope.csv` table must be owned by a named team with a documented refresh cadence and staleness alerting. A minimum operational SLA is: (1) lookup refreshed at a cadence matching the risk domain (daily for access control, weekly for inventory, quarterly for vendor registers), (2) automated alert when the lookup has not been rewritten in 2x the refresh interval, (3) a row-count sanity check (alert if row count changes by more than 20% between refreshes — catches truncation bugs in upstream exports).

Case sensitivity: Splunk's lookup matching is case-sensitive by default. If the raw event field and the lookup key differ in case (e.g. `Account_Name=JSMITH` vs lookup `samaccountname=jsmith`), the join silently returns null. Normalize both sides with `| eval scan_scope = lower(scan_scope)` before the lookup, or set `case_sensitive_match = false` in the lookup's `transforms.conf` stanza. Search across the last 120 days.

**CIM / accelerated variant:** The detection also has an accelerated variant using `| tstats` against the CIM `Vulnerabilities` datamodel:

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t dc(Vulnerabilities.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - agg_value
```

When to prefer this variant: once the underlying datamodel is accelerated (verify in Settings > Data Models > `Vulnerabilities` > status=100%), this query runs 10-50x faster than the raw variant and can feasibly cover multi-month windows for audit reporting. When to avoid: if your field extractions are app-specific and the CIM tagging is incomplete, the `tstats` variant will silently under-count. Always compare raw vs accelerated counts on a known day before swapping the production schedule. Maintain both variants in the UC — the raw is the primary for accuracy, the accelerated is the performance optimisation for dashboarding.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.11.103: PCI-DSS 11.3 — Vulnerability programme: overdue scan cadence]
cron_schedule = 0 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -120d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.11.103,reg=PCI-DSS
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=vm sourcetype=the_sourcetype earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `scan_scope`, `severity`, `state`, `first_seen`, `_time`. Run:

```spl
index=vm sourcetype=the_sourcetype earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table scan_scope, severity, state, first_seen, _time
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(c) Lookup freshness:** The detection joins against `pci_scan_scope.csv`. Check its modification time and row count:

```spl
| inputlookup pci_scan_scope.csv
| eval _lookup_row=1
| stats count as rows
```

Compare the row count to the expected baseline; an unexpected drop is a sign the upstream export truncated.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.11.103: PCI-DSS evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Bullet Chart Of Sla Adherence By Scope (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — Table Of Overdue Scopes (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — Single Value 'Scopes Overdue' (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**No results when violations exist:** Check the lookup's case sensitivity (Splunk lookups are case-sensitive by default) and null handling — a null on the input side produces a silent miss, not an error.

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**PCI-DSS-specific failure modes:**

• Scope creep: PCI Requirement 1.2 requires CDE (Cardholder Data Environment) documentation. Every PCI DSS detection must explicitly reference the CDE inventory lookup; an 'all hosts' search that happens to catch CDE hosts is NOT compliant evidence — the QSA needs to see the scope boundary enforced in the query itself.

• Log retention vs online availability: Req 10.5.1 requires 1 year of audit log retention with at least 3 months immediately available. The index's `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` must be >= 31536000; additionally, Splunk's summary index or an accelerated datamodel must cover the last 90 days for immediate auditor retrieval. One-year hot/warm is not required; the distinction matters for cost.

• Daily log review (Req 10.4): PCI requires DAILY review evidence. Detections backing this control must produce signed evidence per calendar day, not per 24-hour rolling window — a search scheduled at 23:55 daily is compliant; one running every 24h from an arbitrary start time is not.

• FIM (File Integrity Monitoring, Req 11.5.2) scope: FIM detections must cover critical system files on CDE devices, not 'all changes anywhere.' Use the CDE host list as the first-pass filter; flags on non-CDE hosts dilute the signal and are out of scope for the RoC.
grandmaExplanation: We produces, per scan scope, the evidence required by PCI-DSS 11.3 — scans are on cadence and high/critical findings are remediated within SLA. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
